In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.

While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 2]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505年6月26日}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597年10月20日}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年}} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年}} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505年6月26日}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597年10月20日}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2nd year }} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505年6月26日}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2nd year }} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597年10月20日}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2nd year }} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21st year }} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year }} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year }} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14th year }} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2nd year }} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2nd year }} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7th year }} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2nd year }} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21st year }} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year }} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14th year }} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2nd year }} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2nd year }} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7th year }} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2nd year }} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year }} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21st year }} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year }} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14th year }} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

'''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2nd year|12=draft}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=draft}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2nd year|12=draft}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=draft}}]) was a {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|神職|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=draft}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=draft}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=draft}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=draft}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=draft}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=draft}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=draft}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=draft}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7th year|12=draft}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|12=draft}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year|12=draft}} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=draft}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2nd year|12=draft}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year|12=draft}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21st year|12=draft}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=draft}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=draft}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=draft}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year|12=draft}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14th year|12=draft}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

{{citation needed|Moriya Yorizane}}

He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=draft}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|ja|諏訪郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=draft}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=draft}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=draft}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=draft}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya no Kami|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=draft}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=draft}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=draft}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=draft}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7th year|12=draft}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|ja|諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|12=draft}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year|12=draft}} (1542), when {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=draft}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2nd year|12=draft}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11th year|12=draft}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21st year|12=draft}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=draft}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=draft}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=draft}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23rd year|12=draft}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14th year|12=draft}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

revision.text:
'''守矢 頼真'''（もりや よりざね、[[永正]]2年[[5月25日 (旧暦)|5月25日]]〈[[1505年]][[6月26日]]〉- [[慶長]]2年[[9月10日 (旧暦)|9月10日]]（[[1597年]][[10月20日]]〉）は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[神職]]。[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]]に所在する[[諏訪大社]]の[[守矢氏|神長官]]。『諏訪史料叢書』<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>では[[守矢有実]]の子。子は[[守矢信真]]。初名は頼実<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>。
== 略歴 == [[守矢氏]]は[[洩矢神]]あるいは[[物部守屋]]の後裔とも言われるが不明。諏訪大社[[祝 (神職)|大祝]]家の[[諏訪氏]]に従い、たびたび[[禰宜]][[太夫#神道|太夫]]の[[矢島満清]]と対立する。特に諏訪大祝就任の際に争い、当初劣勢だったが、[[天文 (元号)|天文]]7年（[[1538年]]）の[[諏訪頼高]]の大祝就任時に復権した。また諏訪大社への課税をめぐって[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]とも対立した。
天文11年（[[1542年]]）、甲斐国の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は[[信濃侵攻]]を本格化させると諏訪氏との同盟を破棄して諏訪侵攻を開始し、当主頼重は同年7月に甲府へ護送され死去しており、頼真は武田氏の諏訪支配を先導し、戦勝祈願などで信頼を得た（『守矢頼真書留』）。[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に引退し、[[嫡子]]で禰宜太夫に就けていた[[守矢信真|信真]]に神長官を譲った。武田家滅亡後は[[諏訪満隣]]らと諏訪家再興を図る一方、諏訪大社の復興にも力を入れた。
== 守矢頼真書留 == 頼真の自筆書留。全一冊。父の満実の代から書き継いだ諏訪大社の神事や神社経営に関する記事が書き留められており、天文11年（1542年）から天文21年（1552年）分が現存している。書留には天文11年4月の諏訪頼重嫡子[[長岌|寅王丸]]の生誕や同年7月の頼重自刃など、武田側の記録である『[[高白斎記]]』とともに諏訪侵攻に関する基本資料となっているが、天文12年以降は社務中心の記述となっている。原本が守屋家に伝存し、刊本には『[[信濃史料叢書|新編信濃史料叢書]]』第七巻、『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6中世3下（県外記録）などがある。
== 神使御頭之日記 == 享禄元年（1528年）から天文23年（1554年）まで、年ごとに御頭役を務めた神職や郷名を記録している。天文4年（1535年）の武田信虎と諏訪碧雲斎が甲信国境である境川に参会し上社宝錫を鳴らし和平を行ったことをはじめ、晴信期の諏訪侵攻に到るまで関係する事件についても記されている。
守屋家には原本が伝わっていたとされ、『信濃史料』『新編信濃史料史料』においてはこれに基づいて翻刻が行われているが、原本の存在は現在確認されていない。『山資』6では文化14年（1817年）写本に基づいて翻刻している。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:もりや よりさね}} [[Category:戦国時代の人物 (日本)]] [[Category:戦国時代の神職]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の神職]] [[Category:1505年生]] [[Category:1597年没]]

== 略歴 == [[守矢氏]]は[[洩矢神]]あるいは[[物部守屋]]の後裔とも言われるが不明。諏訪大社[[祝 (神職)|大祝]]家の[[諏訪氏]]に従い、たびたび[[禰宜]][[太夫#神道|太夫]]の[[矢島満清]]と対立する。特に諏訪大祝就任の際に争い、当初劣勢だったが、[[天文 (元号)|天文]]7年（[[1538年]]）の[[諏訪頼高]]の大祝就任時に復権した。また諏訪大社への課税をめぐって[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]とも対立した。
天文11年（[[1542年]]）、甲斐国の[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）は[[信濃侵攻]]を本格化させると諏訪氏との同盟を破棄して諏訪侵攻を開始し、当主頼重は同年7月に甲府へ護送され死去しており、頼真は武田氏の諏訪支配を先導し、戦勝祈願などで信頼を得た（『守矢頼真書留』）。[[永禄]]2年（[[1559年]]）に引退し、[[嫡子]]で禰宜太夫に就けていた[[守矢信真|信真]]に神長官を譲った。武田家滅亡後は[[諏訪満隣]]らと諏訪家再興を図る一方、諏訪大社の復興にも力を入れた。
== 守矢頼真書留 == 頼真の自筆書留。全一冊。父の満実の代から書き継いだ諏訪大社の神事や神社経営に関する記事が書き留められており、天文11年（1542年）から天文21年（1552年）分が現存している。書留には天文11年4月の諏訪頼重嫡子[[長岌|寅王丸]]の生誕や同年7月の頼重自刃など、武田側の記録である『[[高白斎記]]』とともに諏訪侵攻に関する基本資料となっているが、天文12年以降は社務中心の記述となっている。原本が守屋家に伝存し、刊本には『[[信濃史料叢書|新編信濃史料叢書]]』第七巻、『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6中世3下（県外記録）などがある。
== 神使御頭之日記 == 享禄元年（1528年）から天文23年（1554年）まで、年ごとに御頭役を務めた神職や郷名を記録している。天文4年（1535年）の武田信虎と諏訪碧雲斎が甲信国境である境川に参会し上社宝錫を鳴らし和平を行ったことをはじめ、晴信期の諏訪侵攻に到るまで関係する事件についても記されている。
守屋家には原本が伝わっていたとされ、『信濃史料』『新編信濃史料史料』においてはこれに基づいて翻刻が行われているが、原本の存在は現在確認されていない。『山資』6では文化14年（1817年）写本に基づいて翻刻している。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:もりや よりさね}} [[Category:戦国時代の人物 (日本)]] [[Category:戦国時代の神職]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の神職]] [[Category:1505年生]] [[Category:1597年没]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|qq=|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. == *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|qq=|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|qq=|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
