He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]]. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in the [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District, Nagano]], [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either [[:en:Moreya|Moreya no Kami]] or [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

Yorizane also opposed [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)]] regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450098]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|en|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|WD=Q2511179|lt=Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--Moriya Yorishin--><!--Moriya Yorishin--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:守矢賴真]] {{wikidata link|Q11450098}} [[Category:1505 births]] [[Category:1597 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:People of Sengoku-period Japan]] [[Category:Shinto Priests of the Azuchi–Momoyama Period]] [[Category:Shinto priests of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=25th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=10th day of the 9th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|en|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|WD=Q2511179|lt=Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.
== Notes ==

== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|en|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|WD=Q2511179|lt=Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=May 25 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=September 10 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku period}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District, Nagano|1=Suwa District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami|1=Moreya}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|en|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|WD=Q2511179|lt=Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|1=Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Q85876578|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

<!--Moriya Yorishin--><!--Moriya Yorishin--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:守矢賴真]] {{wikidata link|Q11450098}} [[Category:1505 births]] [[Category:1597 deaths]] [[Category:People of Sengoku-period Japan]] [[Category:Shinto Priests of the Azuchi–Momoyama Period]] [[Category:Shinto priests of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守矢頼真]][[zh:守矢賴真]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1505|6|26}}|death_date={{death date|1597|10|20}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}} '''Moriya Yorizane''' (守矢 頼真, {{ill|Eishō 2|ja|永正2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|May 25 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月25日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 25 (lunar calendar)|1=May 25 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839582}} [{{ill|June 26, 1505|ja|1505th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 1505|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] – {{ill|Keichō 2|ja|慶長2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|September 10 (lunar calendar)|ja|9月10日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 10 (lunar calendar)|1=September 10 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840113}} [{{ill|October 20, 1597|ja|1597th year 10月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 20, 1597|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]) was a [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priest]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku period}}. He served as the {{ill|jinchōkan|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinchōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (chief priest) of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in the {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District, Nagano|1=Suwa District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. According to the *{{ill|Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja|諏訪史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shiryō Sōsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*<ref>諏訪史料叢書 諏訪教育会著 1925-1952 12巻 守矢頼真書留(守矢頼真) 神長殿知行御検地[https://iss.ndl.go.jp/sp/show/R100000002-I000000871552-00/ 国会図書館]</ref>, he was the son of {{ill|Moriya Ariyoshi|ja|守矢有実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Ariyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His initial name was Yorimitsu<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%AE%88%E7%9F%A2%E9%A0%BC%E5%AE%9F-1794824 コトバンク守矢頼実]参照。</ref>.
== Biography == The {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} is believed to be descended from either {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|WD=Q11146526|lt=Moreya no Kami|1=Moreya}} or {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, but this remains uncertain. Yorizane served under the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, the {{ill|Hōri|lt=Hōri|lt=Ōhori|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}} (chief priests) of Suwa Taisha, and frequently clashed with the {{ill|Negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} {{ill|tayū|ja|太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tayū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大夫|1=Taifu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q14126428}} Yajima Mitsukiyo. He was initially at a disadvantage but regained his position during the appointment of {{ill|Suwa Yoritaka|ja|諏訪頼高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yoritaka|1=Suwa Yoritaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631969}} as Ōhori in {{ill|Tenbun 7|ja|天文7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 7|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1538年|1=1538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6470}} (1538). Yorizane also opposed {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|en|Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)|WD=Q2511179|lt=Suwa Yorishige (Sengoku period)|1=Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)}} regarding taxation on Suwa Taisha.
In {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542), when [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}} initiated his invasion of Suwa, Yorishige was captured and later died. Yorizane supported the Takeda clan’s control of Suwa and earned their trust through rituals and prayers for victory (*{{ill|Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja|守矢頼真書留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Yorizane Shokutō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 守矢頼真|1=Moriya Yorishin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450098}}*). In {{ill|Eiroku 2|ja|永禄2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1559), he retired and passed the role of jinchōkan to his son Nobuzane. After the fall of the Takeda clan, Yorizane worked to revive the Suwa clan and contributed to the restoration of Suwa Taisha.
== *Moriya Yorizane Shokutō* == A personal record written by Yorizane, comprising one volume, documents the rituals and management of Suwa Taisha from his father’s era. The surviving entries span from {{ill|Tenbun 11|ja|天文11年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 11|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1542) to {{ill|Tenbun 21|ja|天文21年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 21|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1552). It includes key historical events, such as the birth of Yorishige’s heir in April 1542 and Yorishige’s death in July. While it initially covers the Takeda invasion of Suwa, later entries focus on shrine administration. The original manuscript was preserved by the Moriya family, and its text has been reproduced in collections such as the *{{ill|Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|ja|信濃史料叢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Shiryō Sōsho|1=Q85876578|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876578}}* and the *{{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja|山梨県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.
== *Shinshi Ontō no Nikki* == A record of the shrine's annual rituals and notable events from {{ill|Kyōroku 1|ja|享禄元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōroku 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1528) to {{ill|Tenbun 23|ja|天文23年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1554). It describes significant occurrences, such as the peace negotiations between Takeda Nobutora and Suwa Hekiunsai at the Sakai River in 1535, and events leading to Takeda Shingen's invasion of Suwa. While the original manuscript has not been located, a copy made in {{ill|Bunka 14|ja|文化14年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka 14|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1817) has been used for reproductions in historical collections.

His son was {{ill|Moriya Nobuzane|ja|守矢信真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Nobuzane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
